# PAI

> PAI is a bootable USB Linux distribution for private, offline AI. Based on
> Debian 12 + Sway + Ollama + Open WebUI, it runs local LLMs entirely on your
> hardware — no cloud, no account, no telemetry. Flash the ISO to a USB stick,
> boot any x86_64 or arm64 machine from it, and you have a complete AI
> workstation that lives in RAM and vanishes when you shut down. Remove the
> stick to return your machine to its previous state.

Repository: https://github.com/nirholas/pai
License: GPL v3
Current version: 0.1.0 (April 2026)
ISO downloads (hosted on Cloudflare at get.pai.direct due to GitHub's 2 GB asset limit):

- amd64: https://get.pai.direct/pai-amd64.iso
- arm64: https://get.pai.direct/pai-arm64.iso
- SHA256: https://get.pai.direct/SHA256SUMS

## Quick start

- [Docs home](/docs/): full navigation of all documentation
- [Installing and booting](/docs/first-steps/installing-and-booting/): flash a USB and boot PAI
- [Using Ventoy](/docs/first-steps/using-ventoy/): boot PAI from a Ventoy multi-boot USB without wiping the drive
- [Starting on Mac](/docs/first-steps/starting-on-mac/): UTM setup for Apple Silicon
- [Starting on Windows](/docs/first-steps/starting-on-windows/): PowerShell flash.ps1 one-liner or VM setup
- [First boot walkthrough](/docs/first-steps/first-boot-walkthrough/): what you see on first launch
- [Desktop basics](/docs/first-steps/desktop-basics/): Sway, waybar, shortcuts
- [Shutting down](/docs/first-steps/shutting-down/): secure shutdown and memory wipe

## General

- [System requirements](/docs/general/system-requirements/): hardware and compatibility
- [Features and included software](/docs/general/features-included/): every app that ships
- [Warnings and limitations](/docs/general/warnings-and-limitations/): honest boundaries
- [How PAI works](/docs/general/how-pai-works/): architecture, stack, boot sequence

## AI and models

- [Using Open WebUI](/docs/ai/using-open-webui/): the chat interface
- [Using Ollama](/docs/ai/using-ollama/): CLI, HTTP API, Python SDK
- [Managing models](/docs/ai/managing-models/): pull, switch, remove
- [Choosing a model](/docs/ai/choosing-a-model/): which model for your hardware

## Privacy

- [Privacy introduction](/docs/privacy/introduction-to-privacy/): threat model + toolbox
- [Privacy mode (Tor)](/docs/privacy/privacy-mode-tor/): system-wide Tor routing
- [Tor Browser](/docs/privacy/tor-browser/): anonymous web browsing
- [MAC anonymization](/docs/privacy/mac-address-anonymization/): hardware ID spoofing
- [Offline mode](/docs/privacy/offline-mode/): fully air-gapped workflow

## Applications

- [Encrypting files with GPG](/docs/apps/encrypting-files-gpg/): symmetric and public-key encryption
- [Password management](/docs/apps/password-management/): KeePassXC on a live system
- [Secure delete](/docs/apps/secure-delete/): permanently erasing files

## Advanced

- [Running in a VM](/docs/advanced/running-in-a-vm/): UTM, VirtualBox, VMware, KVM
- [Building from source](/docs/advanced/building-from-source/): custom ISOs via live-build
- [Boot options](/docs/advanced/boot-options/): GRUB and kernel parameters
- [GPU setup](/docs/advanced/gpu-setup/): NVIDIA, AMD, Intel acceleration
- [GPU passthrough](/docs/advanced/gpu-passthrough/): GPU in a VM via VFIO
- [Troubleshooting](/docs/advanced/troubleshooting/): boot, display, AI, network fixes

- [Keyboard shortcuts](/docs/reference/keyboard-shortcuts/): every shortcut
